item.title: About
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I'm Liza! Welcome to my blog — a place where I share my thoughts on lifestyle, travel, food and fashion. I believe in living beautifully and finding joy in the everyday moments.

This blog is my little corner of the internet where creativity meets simplicity. Whether you're here for recipe inspiration, travel tips, or just a dose of positivity — I'm glad you stopped by!
item.excerpt:encoded: Hi, I'm Liza! Welcome to my blog.

item.title: Contact
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Have a question or a collaboration idea? I'd love to hear from you!

Email: hello@lizablog.com

item.title: 10 Morning Habits That Will Transform Your Day
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
How you start your morning sets the tone for your entire day. Over the years I've experimented with dozens of routines — and these 10 habits have made the biggest difference in my energy, focus and overall happiness.

1. Wake Up Without Your Phone

Give yourself at least 30 minutes before checking social media or emails. Use this time to ease into the day with intention rather than reaction.

2. Hydrate First Thing

Drink a large glass of water before anything else. After 7–8 hours of sleep your body is dehydrated — rehydrating kickstarts your metabolism and clears mental fog.

3. Move Your Body

Even 10 minutes of stretching, yoga, or a short walk outside makes a remarkable difference.

4. Journal for 5 Minutes

Write down three things you're grateful for and one intention for the day.

5. Eat a Nourishing Breakfast

Skip the sugary cereals. Opt for protein, healthy fats and complex carbs to keep your energy stable all morning.
item.excerpt:encoded: How you start your morning sets the tone for your entire day. These 10 habits have made the biggest difference in my energy and focus.

item.title: How to Create a Cozy Home You'll Never Want to Leave
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Creating a cozy, comfortable home isn't about expensive furniture — it's about atmosphere, warmth and the little details that make a space feel truly yours.

Start With Lighting

Harsh overhead lighting is the enemy of cozy. Layer your lighting with floor lamps, table lamps and candles. Warm-toned bulbs instantly make any room feel more inviting.

Add Texture

Pile on the throws, cushions and rugs. Mixing textures adds visual warmth and makes a space feel lived-in and loved.

Bring in Nature

Plants, fresh flowers, dried botanicals — natural elements ground a space and add life.
item.excerpt:encoded: Creating a cozy home isn't about expensive furniture — it's about atmosphere, warmth and the little details that make a space feel truly yours.

item.title: A Simple Guide to Digital Detox
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A digital detox doesn't mean quitting technology forever. It means being intentional about how and when you use it.

Signs You Need a Digital Detox

You reach for your phone first thing in the morning. You feel anxious when you don't have it nearby. You scroll mindlessly without really enjoying it.

How to Start

Start small. Designate phone-free times — during meals, the first hour after waking, and the last hour before bed.
item.excerpt:encoded: A digital detox doesn't mean quitting technology forever. It means being intentional about how and when you use it.

item.title: A Weekend in Tbilisi — The City That Stole My Heart
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tbilisi is one of those cities that sneaks up on you. You arrive expecting a few days of sightseeing and leave completely enchanted — planning your return before you've even unpacked.

Old Town — Kala

Start in the old town, where narrow cobblestone streets wind between colorful wooden balconies draped in grapevines. Every corner reveals a hidden courtyard, a crumbling church, or a tiny wine bar.

The Sulfur Baths of Abanotubani

Soak in private bathhouses fed by natural hot springs. Don't skip the traditional kisi scrub.

Wine, Bread and Cheese

Eat khachapuri for breakfast, try churchkhela as a snack, and drink natural wine poured from a clay qvevri jug.
item.excerpt:encoded: Tbilisi is one of those cities that sneaks up on you. You arrive expecting a few days and leave completely enchanted.

item.title: How to Travel Slow — And Why It Changed Everything
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
I used to think travel meant fitting as many countries as possible into the shortest amount of time. Then I tried slow travel — staying in one place for weeks at a time — and it completely changed the way I see the world.

What Is Slow Travel?

Slow travel is about depth over breadth. Instead of racing through a highlight reel of cities, you settle into one place long enough to find your favourite coffee shop and feel like a local.

The Benefits

Lower costs, less travel stress, deeper cultural immersion, and the unexpected joy of routine in a foreign place.
item.excerpt:encoded: Slow travel changed the way I see the world — staying in one place long enough to find your favourite coffee shop and feel like a local.

item.title: The Ultimate Minimalist Packing List
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
I've traveled with a single carry-on bag for the past three years. No checked luggage fees, no waiting at carousels, no dragging a heavy suitcase over cobblestones.

Clothing (7 items)

The key is a neutral color palette — everything mixes and matches. Two pairs of trousers, three tops, one light jacket, and one versatile dress.

Shoes (2 pairs)

One comfortable walking shoe and one pair of sandals. That's it.

Toiletries

Solid shampoo bars save space. Decant skincare into small reusable bottles. Leave the hair dryer — use the hotel's.
item.excerpt:encoded: I've traveled with a single carry-on bag for three years. No checked luggage fees, no carousel waiting. Here's exactly what I bring.

item.title: One-Pan Lemon Herb Pasta — Ready in 20 Minutes
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
This is my go-to weeknight recipe when I'm tired but still want something that feels like a proper meal. One pan, 20 minutes, minimal washing up.

Ingredients (serves 2)

  • 200g spaghetti
  • 3 cloves garlic, sliced
  • Zest and juice of 1 lemon
  • Large handful of fresh parsley
  • 4 tbsp olive oil
  • 50g parmesan, grated

Method

Cook pasta in salted water. Reserve a cup of pasta water. Fry garlic in olive oil until golden. Add drained pasta, lemon zest, juice and a splash of pasta water. Toss well. Add parmesan and herbs, season and serve.
item.excerpt:encoded: One pan, 20 minutes, minimal washing up. This lemon herb pasta tastes far more impressive than the effort involved.

item.title: My Favourite Everyday Smoothie Bowl
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
I've been making some version of this smoothie bowl almost every morning for two years. It's nourishing, beautiful, and takes less than five minutes.

The Base

Blend 1 frozen banana, a large handful of frozen mixed berries, 2 tablespoons of Greek yogurt and a splash of oat milk until thick and creamy.

Toppings

Granola for crunch, fresh fruit, a drizzle of honey, chia seeds, and fresh mint leaves.
item.excerpt:encoded: I've been making this smoothie bowl almost every morning for two years. Nourishing, beautiful, and ready in five minutes.

item.title: The Best Homemade Bread — No Kneading Required
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
This recipe requires exactly five minutes of active work — the rest is just waiting. The result is a crusty, chewy loaf that rivals anything from a bakery.

Ingredients

  • 400g strong white bread flour
  • 1/4 tsp instant yeast
  • 1.5 tsp salt
  • 300ml warm water

Method

Mix everything until a rough dough forms. Cover and leave at room temperature for 12–18 hours. Shape, rest for another hour, then bake in a preheated Dutch oven at 230°C — 30 minutes covered, then 15 minutes uncovered.
item.excerpt:encoded: This no-knead bread requires five minutes of active work. The result is a crusty, chewy loaf that rivals anything from a bakery.

item.title: How to Build a Capsule Wardrobe From Scratch
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A capsule wardrobe is a curated collection of timeless, versatile pieces that all work together. I rebuilt my wardrobe from scratch two years ago and I've never looked back.

Start With a Clean Slate

Go through what you already own. Try everything on and ask honestly: does this fit well? Have I worn it in the last year? If no — donate or sell.

The Foundation Pieces

Build around white, cream, navy, grey and camel. These colours work together effortlessly. Start with well-fitted trousers, simple shirts, a quality blazer, a classic coat, and comfortable flats.

The Rule of 30

Before buying anything new, ask: will I wear this at least 30 times? If you can't say yes — it doesn't earn a place in your wardrobe.
item.excerpt:encoded: A capsule wardrobe means fewer items but more use from every single one. I rebuilt mine from scratch and never looked back.

item.title: Spring Colour Trends You'll Actually Want to Wear
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Every season I filter the trend colours down to the ones that are actually wearable in everyday life. Here are the spring shades I'm genuinely excited about.

Butter Yellow

Warm, soft and surprisingly versatile. A bag or scarf in butter yellow lifts any neutral outfit instantly.

Sage Green

Sage pairs effortlessly with cream, white, camel and soft pink. A sage linen blazer is top of my wishlist this spring.

Terracotta

Rich, earthy and flattering on warm skin tones. Works brilliantly as a statement coat or unexpected pop of colour through shoes.
item.excerpt:encoded: Every season I filter the trend colours down to the ones actually wearable in everyday life. Here are the spring shades I love.

item.title: The Best Sustainable Fashion Brands to Know Right Now
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Buying less and buying better is the most powerful thing we can do as consumers. Here are the labels I trust and return to.

Everlane

Radical transparency is their brand promise — they show you exactly what each item costs to make. Their cashmere and denim are exceptional quality.

Arket

Beautiful, minimal, built to last. Their linen collection every spring is stunning.

Veja

If you only invest in one sustainable brand, make it Veja for your shoes. Ethically sourced materials, fair wages, no advertising budget.
item.excerpt:encoded: Buying less and buying better is the most powerful thing we can do. Here are the sustainable fashion labels I trust and return to.
